dashboard.label: MCP Access & Tools
dashboard.description: Which accounts act as MCP clients, what they can reach, and which tools they use. Works for the official Splunk MCP Server (capability model) and for custom / community MCP servers (RBAC + user-agent detection).

panel.title: MCP accounts — capability-granted or detected by user-agent

search.query: | rest /services/authentication/users | eval user=title, roles=mvjoin('roles',", "), cap=if(match(mvjoin('capabilities'," "),"mcp_tool_execute"),"mcp_tool_execute",null()) | fields user roles cap | search NOT [ | inputlookup mcp_excluded_users | fields user ] | join type=left user [ search `mcp_rest_clients` | stats count as rest_calls values(client_label) as detected_as by user ] | where isnotnull(cap) OR isnotnull(detected_as) | eval detected_via=mvjoin(mvappend(if(isnotnull(cap),"capability",null()),if(isnotnull(detected_as),"user-agent",null())),", ") | table user roles detected_as detected_via rest_calls | sort - rest_calls

html.div:
Two ways an account is treated as an MCP client:
Official Splunk MCP Server — the role grants the mcp_tool_execute capability; tools come from the catalog and the User × Tool matrix shows ✓ granted / ✗ denied (from mcp_tool_denied.csv).
Custom / community MCP — no such capability; the account is detected by its client user-agent (mcp_user_agents.csv) and tools are inferred from REST endpoints. The matrix then shows ✓ where the account actually used a tool.
Each tool always runs with the account's own Splunk RBAC — the real data boundary is the searchable index scope (below).

panel.title: User × Tool matrix (official: ✓ granted / ✗ denied · custom: ✓ used)

search.query: | inputlookup mcp_tool_catalog | stats values(tool) as tool | appendcols [ | rest /services/authentication/users | search [ | rest /services/authorization/roles | search capabilities=mcp_tool_execute | rename title as roles | fields roles ] | search NOT [ | inputlookup mcp_excluded_users | rename user as title | fields title ] | stats values(title) as user ] | where isnotnull(user) | mvexpand user | mvexpand tool | join type=left user tool [ | inputlookup mcp_tool_denied | eval denied="yes" ] | eval cell=if(denied=="yes","✗","✓") | append [ search `mcp_rest_clients` | `mcp_rest_tool_infer` | stats count by user inferred_tool | rename inferred_tool as tool | eval cell="✓" ] | search NOT [ | inputlookup mcp_excluded_users | fields user ] | chart limit=0 values(cell) over user by tool

panel.title: Tool usage by account (inferred from REST)

search.query: search `mcp_rest_clients` | `mcp_rest_tool_infer` | chart limit=0 count over user by inferred_tool

panel.title: Searchable index scope per MCP account

search.query: | rest /services/authentication/users | eval user=title | fields user roles | mvexpand roles | search [ search `mcp_rest_clients` | stats count by user | fields user | append [ | rest /services/authentication/users | search [ | rest /services/authorization/roles | search capabilities=mcp_tool_execute | rename title as roles | fields roles ] | rename title as user | fields user ] | dedup user ] | search NOT [ | inputlookup mcp_excluded_users | fields user ] | join type=left roles [ | rest /services/authorization/roles | eval roles=title, idx=mvjoin('srchIndexesAllowed',", ") | fields roles idx ] | stats values(roles) as roles values(idx) as searchable_indexes by user
